title: Market Analyst Predicts Bitcoin And Ethereum Prices For The Next 3 Quarters
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Bitcoin is expected to drop below $58,000 in Q2 2026, while Ethereum's future price is not specified. Analysts predict a market panic and S&P 500 decline. Curre
excerpt: Bitcoin is expected to drop below $58,000 in Q2 2026, while Ethereum's future price is not specified. Analysts predict a market panic and S&P 500 decline. Current price is near $80,000.

content.introduction: Bitcoin is expected to drop below $58,000 in Q2 2026, while Ethereum's future price is not specified. Analysts predict a market panic and S&P 500 decline. Current price is near $80,000.
body: Bitcoin is trading close to $80,000 in the first week of May; Jerome Powell is weeks away from stepping down as Federal Reserve chair; the S&P 500 is at an all-time high; and sentiment across crypto markets is slowly turning positive. Crypto trader and market analyst Aralez has stepped forward with a full arc of the industry’s next major cycle that stretches from the second quarter of 2026 into the end of 2027. The first stage of Aralez’s prediction focuses on May and June 2026, where he expects the market to see one more wave of panic. This is the most bearish part of the forecast, and it places the Bitcoin price reaching below $58,000, which would represent a drop of about 27% from its current price near $79,715. Aralez also tied this stage to weakness in the S&P 500, with a prediction that it could reverse and fall below 6,800.

Bitcoin is trading close to $80,000 in the first week of May; Jerome Powell is weeks away from stepping down as Federal Reserve chair; the S&P 500 is at an all-time high; and sentiment across crypto markets is slowly turning positive. Crypto trader and market analyst Aralez has stepped forward with a full arc of the industry’s next major cycle that stretches from the second quarter of 2026 into the end of 2027. The first stage of Aralez’s prediction focuses on May and June 2026, where he expects the market to see one more wave of panic. This is the most bearish part of the forecast, and it places the Bitcoin price reaching below $58,000, which would represent a drop of about 27% from its current price near $79,715. Aralez’s prediction is that the leadership transition will lead to a market sell-off, with the S&P 500 falling to as low as $5,200 in the worst of it.
